courses.name: Art and Society: Prehistory to 1300
courses.description: A survey of art, architecture and artifacts from prehistory to 1300. Ways of understanding visual culture through this span of history.

courses.name: Art and Society: 1300 to the Present
courses.description: A survey of art, architecture and related visual forms in their expanding contexts from 1300 to the present. Ways of understanding visual culture through this span of history.

courses.name: Art as Visual Communication
courses.description: A variety of visual material is organized topically to examine the elements of art (line, shape, value, colour, texture, space), the principles of pictorial organization, the materials and techniques of art, and recurrent tendencies in artistic styles and outlooks.

courses.name: History and Theory of Architecture: Prehistory to 1500
courses.description: An introduction to the history of architecture from prehistory to ca. 1500, considering technological, formal, intellectual and social developments that informed the built environment through a range of building types.

courses.name: History and Theory of Architecture: 1500 to Present
courses.description: An introduction to the history of architecture from ca. 1500 to the present, considering technological, formal, intellectual, and social developments that informed the built environment through a range of building types.

courses.name: Art in Canada
courses.description: Topics may include professional and amateur artists, craftwork, art institutions, gender, nationalism, regionalism, ethnicity, race, and identity. Coverage will include artworks in local and national collections in the National Capital region.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topic: Indigenous Art
courses.description: Survey of an area of indigenous art history. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Asian Art
courses.description: Surveys Asian art from second-century China to post-war Japan. Representational strategies of court artists and artists from the capital are compared with artists on the periphery. Articulation of power in tombs, palaces and war propaganda is examined, as is the individual and the eccentric.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Art Live: Art History Workshop
courses.description: Examination of techniques, materials and institutions of art history; lectures and workshops on art historical research and writing, the materials of art, professional skills; site visits to art institutions.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Greek Art and Archaeology
courses.description: The art, architecture and archaeology of ancient Greece.  Vase painting, sculpture, architecture, town planning and analogous arts are studied.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Roman Art and Archaeology
courses.description: The art, architecture and archaeology of the ancient Romans.  Vase painting, sculpture, architecture, town planning and analogous arts are studied.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Chinese Art and Visual Culture
courses.description: A survey of Chinese art from the pre-modern era to re-inventions of traditions in modern and contemporary art.  Artworks in various media (ink painting, calligraphy, Buddhist sculpture, ceramics, lacquer and garden architecture) will be studied in their historical, cultural and socio-political contexts.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Islamic Architecture and Art
courses.description: Survey of artistic movements in Islamic art and architecture in the Mediterranean, the Near East, and Central and South Asia, from the seventh century to ca. 1450. Commonalities and differences between major dynastic visual cultures will be explored.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics: Art Worlds
courses.description: Survey of an area of global art history.  Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Medieval Architecture and Art
courses.description: A survey of architecture and art in Europe from ca. 313-1500 C.E. Sacred, secular, and domestic works will be discussed with reference to cultural meaning, social function, structure, and form.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Renaissance Art
courses.description: An examination of major works of art and architecture, issues and themes in the Renaissance; emphasis  on the fifteenth and sixteenth centuries, with a look at roots in the fourteenth.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Architecture of the Early Modern World [1400-1750]
courses.description: An examination of architecture from the late medieval period to the 18th century with particular attention paid to architecture and design cultures within the European and Islamic worlds and their cross-cultural interactions.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Art of the 17th and 18th Centuries
courses.description: Tracing developments in 17th- and 18th-century painting, graphic art, sculpture, and architecture. Introduction to artists, art works, and issues central to the relationship between art and society.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Art of the 19th Century
courses.description: Tracing developments in 19th-century painting, graphic art, sculpture, and architecture. Introduction to artists, art works, and issues central to the relationship between art and modernity.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Art in the Global Context Since 1945
courses.description: Art in the global context from 1945 to present, including abstraction, Pop Art, Postmodernism, object art, performance art and installations.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or higher, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Architecture of the 18th and 19th Centuries
courses.description: A survey of key monuments, theories, forms and technological developments of eighteenth- and nineteenth-century architecture.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: European Art 1900-1945
courses.description: Major artistic movements in Europe from about 1900 to 1945.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: History and Theory of Photography
courses.description: Issues, themes, movements in photography and individual photographers from the origins of the medium to the present.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Twentieth-Century Architecture
courses.description: Developments in architectural form and culture through the course of the twentieth century, with emphasis on the formation and subsequent critique of the Modern Movement.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Experiencing Architecture
courses.description: Development of critical thinking, writing, and looking skills in connection to architecture, through a combination of site visits, workshops and classroom exercises.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 1200 and ARTH 1201 or permission of the discipline. Restricted to students in the History and Theory of Architecture B.A. or B.A. Honours program.

courses.name: Philosophy of Art
courses.description: Philosophical approaches to the study of art. Topics such as: the nature of art and artistic value; representation and symbolism in art; art and artifice; art and the emotions; art, culture and ideology; post-structuralism and art; theories of creativity; relationship between artworks and audiences.

courses.name: Themes in Recent and Contemporary Art in Canada
courses.description: Recent and contemporary art in Canada in a variety of media, examined within its social, political, and cultural contexts. Current critical issues will be explored through works in local and national collections in the National Capital region.
courses.prerequisites: Second-year standing, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Canadian Architecture
courses.description: Canadian architecture from the seventeenth century to the present day, covering both stylistic and technological developments. Building styles, methods, and materials in the context of social and economic conditions and construction techniques.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 1100 and ARTH 1101 , or ARTH 1200 and ARTH 1201 , or ARCH 1002 and ARCH 1201, and second-year standing or higher, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Architecture and Representation
courses.description: Examination of the intersections between architecture, representations, and cultures.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 1100 or ARTH 1101 or ARTH 1200 or ARTH 1201 and second-year standing, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Themes in Architecture in Canada
courses.description: Thematically organized course exploring a wide chronological, geographical, and cultural range of sites and design practices in Canada. Topics may include architecture of governance, spaces of mobility, the effect of industry and economy on the designed environment, housing and shelter, tourism, and histories of design.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 1100 or ARTH 1101 or ARTH 1200 or ARTH 1201 and second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Contemporary Chinese Art and Art History
courses.description: Modern and contemporary art in China and beyond from the reform period in 1979 until today. Artworks will be examined in terms of their (art-)historical, discursive, socio-political, infrastructural and transcultural conditions of production and reception.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Studies in Greek Art
courses.description: A study of period or theme in the art and archaeology of Ancient Greece. Topics may vary from year to year. This course is repeatable for credit when the topic changes.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the unit. Permission of the unit required to repeat.

courses.name: Studies in Roman Art
courses.description: A study of a period or theme in the art and archaeology of the ancient Romans.  Topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the unit. Permission of the unit required to repeat.

courses.name: History and Methods of Architectural History
courses.description: The study of the methodologies and research approaches employed by architectural historians.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing or higher in History and Theory of Architecture,  or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: History and Methods of Art History
courses.description: The study of current methodologies and research tools employed by art historians.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing or higher in Art History, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: History of Printmaking
courses.description: Exploration of printmaking techniques from the 16th century to the present focusing on the work of famous and lesser-known printmakers. Topics may include: printmaking genres (from fine art prints to caricature), originality versus reproduction, book illustration, the art market, posters and propaganda.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or higher, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Digital Media Production for Emerging Arts Professionals
courses.description: Hands-on introduction to media productions tools, techniques and concepts for students planning careers in the arts sector or related fields. Topics may include website development, design and image editing, audio (podcasting) or video, digital photography, writing for the web and integration with other media.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Contemporary Art in the Global Context
courses.description: Contemporary art in the global context.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or higher, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Art and Architecture on Site
courses.description: The study of art and/or architecture on site outside the National Capital Region, in Canada or internationally. May include a combination of study in Ottawa and on site. Locations vary. Students are expected to bear all travel and other costs arising from site visits.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the Discipline.  Applicants will normally have third-year standing with a minimum of 1.0 credit in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture and a GPA of 8.0 or above.

courses.name: Selected Museum Exhibition
courses.description: This seminar complements a major exhibition held at a specific museum. Students enrolled in this course are expected to bear all travel and other costs arising from required visits to the museum.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or higher or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics: Cities in Context
courses.description: Architecture and designed environment of cities. Topics may include comparative studies of cities and the built world across time and geography, theories and histories of urban form and planning, and cultures of placemaking. Topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 1100 or ARTH 1101 or ARTH 1200 or ARTH 1201 and second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Art and Visual Culture
courses.description: Selected aspects of art history and visual culture from ancient times to the present. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics about the Designed Environment
courses.description: Selected aspects of the history of the designed environment, from ancient times to the present. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 1100 or ARTH 1101 or ARTH 1200 or ARTH 1201 and second-year standing or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Practicum in Art and Architectural History
courses.description: Practical experience gained by working on specific projects under the supervision of the staff of a museum, cultural institution, public- or private-sector organization associated with art, architecture, design, or heritage. A maximum of 1.0 credit in practicum courses may be used to fulfill program requirements.
courses.prerequisites: B.A. or B.A. (Honours) in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture with third-year standing or higher and a CGPA of 9.00 or better in ARTH courses, and permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Art in Canada
courses.description: Special topics in art in Canada may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website. Students will be exposed to works in local and national collections in the National Capital region.
courses.prerequisites: one of ARTH 2002 , ARTH 2003, ARTH 3000 and fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Architecture in Canada
courses.description: Special topics about the designed environment in Canada. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Contemporary Art
courses.description: Critical examination of contemporary art. Topics may include socially engaged art, historiographies of contemporary art, re-inventions of traditions, gender and politics of the body, exhibition histories and infrastructures of contemporary art. Topics may vary from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Contemporary Indigenous Art
courses.description: This course will use critical theory to examine aspects of contemporary visual art created by the Inuit and First Peoples in North America. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 2004 and fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Global Art
courses.description: Histories and theories of global art. Topics may include transnational theories of cultural analysis, Orientalism, Post-Colonial theory, translation theory and theories of cultural hybridity. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Islamic Architecture and Art
courses.description: Topics in Islamic Architecture and Art may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 2107 or ARTH 2310 and fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Art, Architecture, and Gender
courses.description: Art and/or architectural creation, reception and/or historiography through the lens of gender identities. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in the Theory and History of Photography
courses.description: Relates the themes of selected theoretical texts on photography to specific examples of photographic practice. Topics may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 2601 and fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Modern Architecture or Design
courses.description: Topics in architecture and design of the Modern era may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: ARTH 2610 and fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Art and Architecture on Site
courses.description: Intensive study of art and/or architecture on site outside the National Capital region, in Canada or internationally. May include a combination of study in Ottawa and on site.  Students are expected to bear all travel and other costs arising from site visits.
courses.prerequisites: Permission of the Discipline.  Applicants will normally have fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture and a CGPA of 8.0 or above.

courses.name: Seminar: Selected Museum Exhibition
courses.description: Studies a major exhibition held at a specific museum. Students enrolled in this course are expected to bear all travel and other costs arising from required visits to the museum.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture and permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Architectural History
courses.description: Topics in architectural history from ancient times to the present may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Special Topics in Historical Western Art
courses.description: Special topics in Western art from the medieval period to the 20th century may vary from year to year, and will be posted on the School for Studies in Art and Culture website.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Topics in Art History and Criticism
courses.description: Selected aspects of art history and/or criticism from ancient times to the present.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture, or permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Directed Readings and Research
courses.description: Supervised readings and research projects. Guidelines must be obtained from the Undergraduate Supervisor prior to registration. A written project outline, approved by the supervising Art History or History and Theory of Architecture faculty member, must be submitted by the last day for course changes.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture and permission of the Discipline.

courses.name: Honours Research Project
courses.description: A project resulting from independent research, supervised by Art History or History and Theory of Architecture faculty. The medium of presentation will be agreed upon between student and supervisor and may include a research paper, web-based project, or combination of dissemination activities.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Art History or History and Theory of Architecture with a minimum CGPA of 10.00 and permission of the Discipline.

free_electives.degree: Art History B.A. Combined Honours (20.0 credits)
free_electives.text: 6.  Sufficient free electives to make 20.0 credits in total for the program.

requirements.description: Art and Society: 1300 to the Present
History and Theory of Architecture: 1500 to Present
History and Methods of Art History

programs.degree: Art History B.A. Combined Honours (20.0 credits)

requirements.description: Art and Society: 1300 to the Present
History and Methods of Art History
a.  0.5 credit in ARTH (excluding ARTH 4900 , ARTH 4909 )
Art and Society: 1300 to the Present
History and Theory of Architecture: 1500 to Present
History and Methods of Art History
Art and Society: 1300 to the Present

programs.degree: Post-Baccalaureate Diploma in Art History (4.0 credits)

requirements.description: History and Methods of Art History
2.5 credit in ARTH at the 2000-level or above (excluding ARTH 2009 )
